title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson face assault charges for allegedly beating a man after a parking accident in Dorchester.
The incident occurred at Blue Hill Avenue and Abbot Street; the victim sustained facial injuries and was hospitalized.
Shearrion and Swinson were arraigned in Boston Municipal Court, pleaded not guilty, and are held at Suffolk County Jail.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox aim to build on their 2025 postseason return with changes to their starting rotation and lineup.
Key players like Roman Anthony and Bryan Bello are expected to lead improvements amid questions about run production and defense.
Manager Alex Cora faces challenges adjusting the lineup due to infield defensive issues and an outfield/DH player logjam.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced 'Match-Ready Massachusetts,' a public safety plan for seven 2026 World Cup matches in Foxborough.
Four special event trains will run between South Station and Foxborough Station during Thursday's Brazil vs. France match.
A FIFA Fan Festival in Boston will feature live broadcasts, games, and food during the World Cup matches.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The New England Revolution supports the White Stadium renovation, paralleling its Everett stadium project as beneficial to communities.
Boston Legacy FC's $190 million partnership for White Stadium is challenged, citing violation of Massachusetts Constitution Article 97.
Opponents of the renovation appeal to the Supreme Judicial Court, arguing the stadium development misuses public park land.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford, Maine man is in critical condition after being struck while helping a stranded driver on I-95.
The incident occurred Monday night in Veazie, Maine, amid snowy conditions causing two vehicles to lose control.
Police report the man may have been shielding an 18-year-old woman, who suffered minor injuries from the accident.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Rohane, 60, was arrested following a suspicious fire at his Monterey residence early Wednesday.
No injuries were reported at the fire which occurred at 340 Main Road in Monterey, Massachusetts.
State Police located Rohane after searching and took him into custody, later sending him to a hospital.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An 11-year-old Labrador named Tessa was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth using pepperoni as bait.
The sinkhole in Tessa's backyard likely formed from a leak in an underground pipe that eroded surrounding soil.
Falmouth Fire and Rescue crews used a pole leash and stable surface setup to rescue Tessa after a two-hour operation.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue in New England is rehabilitating 16 axolotls with missing limbs and infections from overcrowding.
The axolotls, nicknamed 'cheese babes,' are receiving veterinary care and fostering; Burrata was adopted to a family in Florida.
Donations and fosters are needed to support long-term care and exotic vet expenses for the axolotls regrowing their limbs.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein discusses how imperfections in objects can symbolize personal growth and relationship lessons.
A24 hosts a free preview screening of 'The Drama' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson in Somerville.
Yowei Shaw shares a story of a burnt table as evidence of a relationship lesson on the Love Letters podcast.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opened a student-run salon with 25 stations for hands-on cosmetology training.
Students can gain practical experience on public clients, working towards 1,000 training hours for state licensure.
Appointments for services are required and can be made via email or phone; all supervised by instructors.
